page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear tradeoff between memory capacity and processing speed. The instance demonstrates weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, placing it in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Similarly, single-threaded compression and decompression tasks register poor performance in the bottom 10%. However, the server achieves average performance in large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations, as well as Redis SET operations, which reach 5.94 million operations per second. Small-model LLM inference prompt processing and text generation also fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.se1.2xlarge offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU throughput. Due to the lack of local storage and weak database operation benchmarks, it is best utilized for in-memory caching, Redis deployments, light web serving, and workloads requiring moderate memory capacity without heavy parallel processing demands.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 8.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results reveal a distinct tradeoff: while large-block memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations perform at average levels, CPU-intensive tasks, single-core processing, and database operations score in the bottom 25%. Consequently, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, compute-light workloads. It is best suited for in-memory caching, Redis key-value stores, and light web serving applications that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without requiring high-speed local disk access or rapid CPU execution.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8163 processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate compute.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server offering high memory density for caching and key-value databases without local storage requirements.
